MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-19-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEC-02-2020 HEARING HELD RE: PETITIONER STEPHEN ZIMMERMAN'S PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE AND OR ORDINARY MANDAMUS; REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DECISION (CCP 632, 1060, 1021.5, 1085,1094.5, GOVT CODE 800). THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER ZIMMERMAN'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED IN PART AND THE CASE IS REMANDED FOR RESPONDENT TO RE-CALCULATE "[T]HAT PORTION" OF THE SUMS THAT THE CITY CONTRIBUTED TO PETITIONER'S DISABILITY RETIREMENT ALLOWANCE AND REDUCE OR OFFSET THAT AMOUNT AGAINST WHAT THE CITY PAID UNDER WORKERS' COMPENSATION LAW. (SAN FRANCISCO CHARTER SEC. A8.597-7.) STATEMENT OF DECISION SIGNED. (SEE SIGNED STATEMENT OF DECISION FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS) (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative And Or Ordinary Mandamus; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Costs And Attorney Fees; Declaration Of Counsel In Support Thereof; Request For Statement Of Decision (Ccp 632, 1060, 1021.5, 1085,1094.5, Govt Code 800)
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ELLEN MENDELSON, Esq. of Law Office of Ellen Mendelson, P.C. (Tel: 415-585-0815) appearing for Petitioner Stephen Zimmerman;
Atty. NANCY J. TAVERNIT, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-3961) appearing for Respondents San Francisco Employees' Retirement System, Retirement Board of the City and County of San Francisco, and the City and County of San Francisco.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER STEPHEN ZIMMERMAN'S Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative And Or Ordinary Mandamus; Request For Statement Of Decision (Ccp 632, 1060, 1021.5, 1085,1094.5, Govt Code 800).

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Petitioner Zimmerman's petition for writ of mandate is granted in part and the case is remanded for respondent to re-calculate "[t]hat portion" of the sums that the City contributed to petitioner's disability retirement allowance and reduce or offset that amount against what the City paid under workers' compensation law. (San Francisco Charter sec. A8.597-7.) (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Statement of Decision signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative And Or Ordinary Mandamus; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Costs And Attorney Fees; Declaration Of Counsel In Support Thereof; Request For Statement Of Decision (Ccp 632, 1060, 1021.5, 1085,1094.5, Govt Code 800)
Off Calendar Nov-19-2020 Continued To Dec-02-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER STEPHEN ZIMMERMAN's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative And Or Ordinary Mandamus; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Request For Costs And Attorney Fees; Declaration Of Counsel In Support Thereof; Request For Statement Of Decision (Ccp 632, 1060, 1021.5, 1085,1094.5, Govt Code 800):

Continued to December 2, 2020. Petitioner shall e-mail a copy of the administrative record to Contestdept302tr@sftc.org immediately.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
